input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS, targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version:** Install the latest stable release. MAUI's release notes specify the minimum Xcode version per .NET release.
- **Post-install:**
  ```bash
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch   # installs CLI tools & simulators
  xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version` → shows version and build number.

---

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — download the latest **LTS** or **STS** SDK for macOS (Arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).
- **Discover versions:** The download page always highlights the recommended SDK. The [release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/core/tree/main/release-notes) list which MAUI workload ships with each SDK.
- **Verify:**
  ```bash
  dotnet --version        # SDK version
  dotnet --list-sdks      # all installed SDKs
  ```

---

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

- **Install:**
  ```bash
  sudo dotnet workload install maui
  ```
  This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and all transitive dependencies (Android SDK, build tools, etc.).
- **Discover what's available:**
  ```bash
  dotnet workload search          # lists available workloads
  dotnet workload list            # lists currently installed workloads
  ```
- **Verify:**
  ```bash
  dotnet workload list            # should show "maui" as installed
  ```

---

## 4. Android SDK & Java (handled automatically)

The MAUI workload auto-provisions the Android SDK and OpenJDK under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/`. You generally don't need a separate install, but if you want manual control:

- **Android Command-Line Tools:** [developer.android.com/studio#command-tools](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools)
- **Set env vars (if customizing):**
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  export JAVA_HOME="/usr/libexec/java_home"
  ```
- **Accept licenses:**
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
    -f net9.0-android \
    "-p:AndroidSdkDirectory=$ANDROID_HOME" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
  *(Adjust the TFM like `net10.0-android` to match your SDK version.)*

---

## 5. IDE (pick one)

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (discontinued — legacy only) | N/A |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

VS Code with the C# Dev Kit + MAUI extension is now the officially recommended path on macOS.

---

## 6. End-to-End Verification

```bash
# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run \
  -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=SIMULATOR_UDID

# Run on Android Emulator (create one first via AVD manager)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

If both builds succeed and the app launches, your environment is fully working.

---

## 7. Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| Question | Command / URL |
|----------|---------------|
| Which .NET SDK versions exist? | `dotnet --list-sdks` or [dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet) |
| Which MAUI version ships with my SDK? | `dotnet workload list` + [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) |
| Which Xcode does my MAUI need? | [MAUI supported platforms docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms) |
| Which Android API levels are installed? | `dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n` |

Adjust all `net9.0-*` TFMs to match the major .NET version you install (e.g., `net10.0-*` for .NET 10).
